title: shiia lutchuensis
intro: Shiia lutchuensis, also known as Japanese pepper or Japanese prickly ash, is a type of deciduous shrub native to East Asia. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of lignans and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and acting as an anti-inflammatory agent. Shiia lutchuensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and gastrointestinal problems.
